findings.description: Caching the fused OUTPUT (not just inputs) made compile-time LLM fusion deterministic in CI — re-fusion fires only on a source-hash change, and `guild compile --check` verifies drift with zero API calls. The load-bearing architectural bet of the whole rebuild paid off.
findings.evidence: PLAN 2.1 risk (fusion non-determinism); the check-and-smoke phase + no-op-re-run smoke test.

findings.description: The antagonist panels caught substance, not nits: a tautological assertion (expect(x).toBe(x)) rewritten to a meaningful one tied to writtenAgentCount, and a recipeVerb signature non-conformance with the dispatcher type contract.

The installed/cached `guild` CLI lagged the repo and bit repeatedly — the PATH binary predated the compile/recipe/generate verbs (unknown-verb), forcing the repo-local `node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts` fallback. Tier-2 preflight validates loom doctor readability but not guild's verb table, so the gap surfaced only at smoke time (and recurred in guild-workflow-coverage days later).

The freshness gate's regen cascade was not budgeted into the unit contract: source-fragment edits inline into the committed agents at generate-time, so the gate fires on every source edit. U2's disqualifier #6 ('no file outside modes/domains/') was naive about this; 9 regen modifications were unanticipated and AC #6 was amended mid-execution to carve them out.

A stacked-PR merge race silently dropped a schema-validator test from main — PR #119 merged at 18:18:13Z while U2's commit (955484f) pushed at 18:18:29Z, so axes-schema.test.ts never landed via #119. Recovered out-of-band via git cherry-pick (d74bb63).

The minimal guild TOML reader doesn't support nested sub-tables under array-of-tables, discovered mid-execution. The cache shape had to flatten from [cells.source_hashes] sub-tables to flat source_hash_<axis> keys to honor the reader's grammar rather than expanding toml.ts.

Extend the dispatch preflight to validate guild's verb table against the repo's expected VERBS, not just loom doctor manifest readability. The cached-CLI lag recurred because nothing checks guild CLI freshness before a codegen dispatch — turning a smoke-time surprise into a preflight catch.
findings.evidence: Recurrence across labeled-fragments#02, delete-old-codegen#01, and guild-workflow-coverage.

findings.description: Contracts that edit codegen SOURCE (modes/ fragments, axes.toml) must pre-declare the regen cascade in their file-set and disqualifier carve-out, so the freshness-gate consequence is contract-authoring convention rather than a mid-execution amendment.

Open-Q4's before/ voice snapshot was skipped — no quantitative old-vs-new voice diff exists; the pre-fusion voice is now only recoverable from git history. The qualitative learning did land separately, so nothing was lost intellectually.
findings.evidence: PLAN Open-Q4; before/ dir absent; learnings/session-notes/2026-05-28-llm-fusion-voice-convergence.md present.

findings.description: PLAN.md carries a stale doc path — it cites docs/AGENT-CODEGEN.md but the doc lives at plugins/guild/docs/AGENT-CODEGEN.md (plugin-canonical). Flagged as a notes_for_pr follow-up during execution, never reconciled.
Build the guild verb-table preflight check named in the process-change finding — track the recurring cached-CLI-lag friction as concrete substrate work rather than a standing observation.
findings.evidence: Process-change finding above; see also the guild-codegen-cache-skew memory.
